# INTUNE Security Baseline 与策略冲突 — 已知问题详情

**条目数**: 36 | **生成日期**: 2026-04-17

---

## Quick Troubleshooting Path

### Step 1: Intune Endpoint Security App Control for Business policy returns error 65000 when deploying a template generated by WDAC Wizard; the same template ...
**Solution**: Edit the WDAC XML template: (1) Remove <PolicyTypeID> element, (2) Add <PolicyID> with a unique GUID (generate via PowerShell: New-Guid), (3) Set <BasePolicyID> to the same GUID if no base policy exists, (4) Re-upload to Intune. Verify by checking if {GUID}.CIP file appears in C:\windows\system32\codeintegrity\CIPolicies\Active\.
`[Source: onenote, Score: 9.0]`

### Step 2: 部署 Security Baseline 后无法通过 RDP 远程访问 PC，或 Miracast 投屏功能被阻止
**Solution**: RDP 问题参考: https://internal.evergreen.microsoft.com/en-us/topic/a1703643-7bcc-5cc6-810a-80299e59ceae。Miracast 问题参考: https://internal.evergreen.microsoft.com/en-us/topic/3a148351-3457-4471-8e29-ecfc5e36db2f。检查 Security Baseline 中的相关设置并根据业务需求调整
`[Source: ado-wiki, Score: 9.0]`

### Step 3: Security baseline shows 'Pending' status; device never receives the policy
**Solution**: 1) Confirm device last check-in is recent (force sync if needed). 2) Verify user/device is direct member of assigned group. 3) Check Azure deviceId/objectId matches group membership in ASC. 4) Troubleshoot effective group membership: https://internal.evergreen.microsoft.com/en-us/topic/63526f19-9ef6-9b79-d381-d8ac8dda1ee5
`[Source: ado-wiki, Score: 9.0]`

### Step 4: Security baseline profile shows 'Pending' status — device not receiving or applying security baseline policy
**Solution**: 1) Confirm device last check-in status is recent and force sync 2) Confirm user/device is direct member of assigned group 3) Verify Azure deviceId/objectId matches group membership in ASC 4) Troubleshoot effective group membership (internal KB 63526f19-9ef6-9b79-d381-d8ac8dda1ee5)
`[Source: ado-wiki, Score: 9.0]`

### Step 5: Endpoint Security support boundary reference — which teams own which security features when setting applies correctly but feature doesn't work as e...
**Solution**: Support boundaries: Defender Application Guard → Windows UEX | Firewall → Windows Networking | SmartScreen → Azure Security | Encryption → MSaaS Windows Devices | Exploit Guard/ASR → Azure Security | Application Control/Credential Guard → MSaaS Windows Devices | Security Center → Azure Security | Antivirus → Azure Security | Account Protection → MSaaS (credential guard) or Azure Identity (WHFB). All Windows ARs must first post in SME channel with WindowsAR tag
`[Source: ado-wiki, Score: 9.0]`

### Step 6: Security Baseline last report time in Intune portal does not update in sync with device last check-in time; customer expects them to match.
**Solution**: This is expected behavior. Inform customer: (1) Report data refreshes every 24 hours; (2) If no changes detected, delta report refreshes every 7 days; (3) Last check-in time reflects MDM sync, not report data refresh.
`[Source: onenote, Score: 8.5]`

### Step 7: When trying to install the Microsoft Intune Exchange Connector the following error is seen: The Microsoft Intune Exchange Connector experienced an ...
**Solution**: On the Exchange Connector machine: 1) Install .NET 4.5.2. 2) Add DWORD SchUseStrongCrypto under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\.NETFramework4.0.30319 and HKLM\SOFTWARE\Wow6432Node\Microsoft\.NETFramework4.0.30319 to ensure TLSv1.2. 3) Install Windows security patch 2960358 to disable unsafe RC4 cipher in .NET TLS.
`[Source: contentidea-kb, Score: 7.5]`

### Step 8: If a customer created a security baseline between 12/15 and 12/17 and were not flighted into the private preview, they will soon not be able to see...
**Solution**: Request flighting of Security Baselines through the normal flighting request process. The flighting tag is �EnableSecurityBaseline�
`[Source: contentidea-kb, Score: 7.5]`
